game_name: Gabriel Knight: Sins of the Fathers

design_philosophy.core_loop: Investigation-driven narrative progression: visit locations, talk to characters using context-specific dialogue options, collect inventory items, examine clues, and advance through linear chapter-based story with optional side actions
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mystery investigation with consequence-driven gameplay—failure in dangerous situations triggers game-over, forcing reload; player must discover voodoo-related murders while uncovering family supernatural legacy
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a horror novelist-turned-paranormal investigator solving occult mysteries in New Orleans while discovering secret family history as a 'Schattenjäger' (Shadow Hunter)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict point-and-click adventure with action-specific cursor icons (WALK, LOOK, ASK, TALK, etc.) rather than context-sensitive interaction; story-first design prioritizing narrative over mechanical challenge; optional side actions rewarded through scoring system that encourages thorough exploration and dialogue without blocking progress; linear chapter structure with non-linear exploration within each day; failure states exist for dangerous situations but are avoidable through careful gameplay

difficulty.continues_system: Game-over on failure requires retry, restart, or load previous save
difficulty.checkpointing: Save system available; chapter-based structure with required actions per chapter
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can avoid most dangerous situations through careful action selection; no difficulty settings mentioned; optional actions allow players to deepen understanding without time pressure

progression.unlock_mechanism: Chapter-based progression; each chapter requires completing a set of mandatory actions to advance to next day; locations unlock via overhead map navigation; topics unlock in ASK conversations as players discover information
replayability_hooks: Optional side actions provide deeper lore insights not required for progression
Conversation topics divided into Global and Specific categories encourage replaying dialogue
RECORDER icon allows revisiting past conversations to find missed details
Scoring system tracks both required and optional actions, incentivizing completionist playstyle

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through voodoo-influenced New Orleans setting
Character-driven dialogue with context-specific ASK conversations revealing backstory
Inventory item examination for narrative detail
Gabriel's narration when examining items or witnessing events
Historical voodoo mythology integration (both Americanized Louisiana and West African versions)
Mystery plot structure across linear chapter progression covering family supernatural heritage
Multi-location exploration (New Orleans, Rittersburg Germany, Benin) revealing global scope of Shadow Hunters

faction_indicators: Schattenjägers (Shadow Hunters): secret supernatural defense organization with mysterious origins
Voodoo practitioners: antagonistic force using occult for evil
New Orleans detective/police: institutional investigation authority
danger_signals: Supernatural entities: ghosts, cultists, demons throughout history
Family legacy of supernatural combat tied to player character's identity
environmental_storytelling: New Orleans voodoo culture and history
Rittersburg, Germany: ancestral homeland of Shadow Hunters
Urban investigation locations connected to murder mystery

sources_used: wikipedia: Gabriel Knight: Sins of the Fathers
